factors.summary: The company has a strong balance sheet with manageable debt leverage, supported by positive cash flows.

evidence.text: The Company has access to most global markets at a reasonable cost and expects to have sufficient sources of global funding and liquidity to meet its funding needs.

factors.summary: Free cash flow has been positive and stable, contributing to the company's liquidity.

evidence.text: Positive cash flows from consolidated operating activities in the first nine months of 2016 were $1,320 million.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to increase slightly, indicating confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: Property and equipment cash expenditures for the equipment operations in the first nine months of 2016 were $385 million, compared with $458 million in the same period last year. Capital expenditures for the equipment operations in 2016 are estimated to be approximately $650 million.

factors.summary: The company is returning value to shareholders through dividends, though buyback activity has decreased.

evidence.text: Dividends declared during the first nine months of 2016 totaled $1,500 million, compared with $1,471 million in the same period last year.
